feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Community
feed.subtitle: SAP Community blog posts in SAP Community

entry.title: 🎤 Palestras confirmadas para Recife, 10 de maio até o momento!
entry.content:
🎤Palestras confirmadas para Recife, 10 de maio!
Garanta sua participação! pelo link: SAP Inside Track Recife 2025 #SITREC2025

💥Seja um palestrante - Ainda dá tempo! 💥
Realize sua inscrição como palestrante e submeta seu tema para análise

entry.title: SAP CodeJam HANA ML in India 2025-03 Recap
entry.content:
At the beginning of March 2025, as part of the ML/AI Tour my fellow dev advocate @noravonthenen and I did in India, we held five SAP CodeJam events in Kolkata, Pune, and Bengaluru.

Two of these events focused on getting started with Machine Learning using SAP HANA and Python.

Equally important were chances to catch up with some organizers and participants in informal post-work settings...

...or with our SAP colleagues.

I am glad Nora and I could help so many participants to go through the journey of improving their skills working with ML and AI!

Traditionally, I very much enjoyed SAP Stammtisch at the end of the last CodeJam of the Tour.

#IamSAP 

I always take the opportunity to experience at least a little bit of the city I am in, so you can see my photos:

  • Kolkata: https://www.instagram.com/p/DG1wL0OydCp/ 
  • Pune: https://www.instagram.com/p/DG75rf0MPkt/ 

Now, the next stop for me is in Kraków on April 3rd.

Would you like to host such an SAP CodeJam? Here are the details: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/quot-getting-started-with-machine-learning-usin...

entry.title: Wollongong Stammtisch Recap March '25
entry.content:
It was great have a crowd of 15 or so in attendance for the very first SAP Stammtisch Wollongong.  That included 5 super keen attendees who came down from Sydney. They received a warm welcome from the local crew.

There are a few folks, like John P, who often trek up to Sydney for Stammtisch. I'm glad we had chance to return the favour.

Everyone soon got the hang of things, with old friends catching up and introductions being made for those who hadn't met before. We had a beautiful day in a beautiful city.

I believe we may also have witnessed a world first for SAP Stammtisch, with Mark and Simon taking time out to go for an ocean swim. Sadly we don't have any photographic evidence. We will try to rectify that in the next edition. 

Should that be in a year or so?

Thanks to everyone who came, and especially Mark who booked the great venue. Lets spread the word so that next year we might have even more attendees

entry.title: Resumo do SAP Inside Track RJ 2025 – Um Evento de Sucesso no Rio de Janeiro
entry.content:
Falando na minha participação, e do @Jonys_Arcanjo  no SAP Inside Track RJ 2025 ocorreu no ultimo sábado em um hotel localizado em Botafogo, com uma vista deslumbrante para a Baía de Guanabara. O evento contou com um anfiteatro lotado, destacando o alto nível de engajamento da comunidade SAP. A organização foi impecável, proporcionando um ambiente agradável com coffee-breaks pela manhã e à tarde, camisetas exclusivas do evento para todos os participantes e brindes oferecidos por patrocinadores.

O grande diferencial do evento foram as palestras altamente interativas, promovendo discussões enriquecedoras entre os participantes sobre temas relevantes da área técnica e funcional. Entre os tópicos que geraram mais debate estavam Clean Core, SAP Grow e a implementação do DRC. O mais interessante foi a diversidade de perspectivas, onde cada argumento fazia sentido dentro do seu contexto.

A programação contou com apresentações de alto nível, incluindo:

 

  • Manuel Robalinho, trazendo inovações sobre conectividade na área financeira e fluxo de caixa com IA;
  • Jonys Arcanjo, abordando novidades no SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC);
  • Marssel Vilaça, falando sobre o engajamento da Comunidade SAP;
  • Renan Correa, trazendo insights sobre DRC e sua implementação;
  • Fabio Dezan, com uma excelente palestra sobre Localização Brasil;
  • Luciano Casado, explicando a integração de Apps com SAP Grow;
  • João Secco, aprofundando o conceito de Clean Core;
  • Ely Barbosa, com uma aula prática e divertida sobre gestão de projetos e os erros comuns que devem ser evitados.

 

Após um dia repleto de aprendizado e troca de experiências, os participantes encerraram o evento em um bar próximo, onde seguiram a conversa em um ambiente descontraído.

Parabéns à organização por proporcionar um evento tão enriquecedor para a comunidade SAP no Rio de Janeiro! 🚀👏

entry.title: Tech Day Nova Consulting
entry.content:
Olá Pessoal, passando por aqui para compartilhar com vocês um evento incrível que acontecerá essa semana aqui em Curitiba!!

Tech Day Nova Consulting: Tudo o que você precisa saber sobre a migração para o SAP S/4HANA Cloud!
A contagem regressiva para o fim do suporte ao SAP ECC já começou. E agora, qual o melhor caminho para as empresas? Migrar já ou esperar até 2030? Qual estratégia adotar para minimizar riscos e maximizar o retorno sobre o investimento?

Essas e outras dúvidas serão respondidas no Tech Day, um evento exclusivo, gratuito e imperdível, promovido pela Nova Consulting. No dia 20 de março de 2025, a Nova reunirá especialistas do setor para um debate aprofundado sobre os desafios e oportunidades da migração para o SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

📌O que esperar do evento?
Durante o Tech Day, você terá acesso a painéis estratégicos que vão abordar:
✔ Quando migrar para o SAP S/4HANA Cloud? Agora ou até 2030?
✔ Como evitar riscos e garantir uma migração segura.
✔ Greenfield vs. Brownfield – Qual estratégia escolher?
✔ Os custos reais e como otimizar o ROI da transição.
✔ Casos reais de empresas que já fizeram a migração.
💬Além disso, teremos uma sessão interativa de perguntas e respostas com especialistas!

🎯Por que participar?
Conteúdo atualizado: informações diretas de especialistas do mercado.
Networking estratégico: conecte-se com profissionais e empresas que estão passando pelo mesmo desafio.
Formato híbrido: participe de onde estiver!

📅Detalhes do evento:
📆Data: 20/03/2025
📍Formato: Presencial e on-line
💰Evento 100% gratuito!

🚀Garanta sua inscrição e esteja preparado para o futuro do SAP!

🔗 https://conteudos.novaconsulting.com.br/quero_participar 

#SAPCommunityCWB #SAP #TechDay #SAPS4HANA #TransformaçãoDigital #NovaConsulting

entry.title: Why IDoc is Triggered Even After Setting Filters in BD64?
entry.content:
Introduction

Filtering IDocs in BD64 (Distribution Model) helps ensure only relevant data is sent to the target system. However, IDocs may still be generated despite applied filters, leading to confusion. This behavior aligns with standard SAP functionality.

Understanding the Issue

Even after setting filters in BD64, SAP may still trigger IDocs when filter conditions don’t match. This occurs due to:

  • Certain message types bypassing filters.
  • Configuration inconsistencies.
  • Standard SAP logic that processes IDocs before applying filters.

How to Prevent Unwanted IDocs

To avoid unnecessary IDoc generation:

  • Verify BD64 filter settings.
  • Check for SAP Notes addressing this behavior.

Where to check the segments is mandatory.

  • Go to Transaction code WE30.

  • Select objects name.

  •   After going inside the message type you can see this

 

  • Expand the Segments.

  • Double clicks the segments which you want to keep it as filter.

                 

 Key Consideration: Mandatory Checkbox in BD64

The Mandatory checkbox in BD64 plays a crucial role in filtering IDocs. SAP’s standard behavior removes non-mandatory segments but still allows IDoc transmission, which can lead to unexpected IDocs being generated.

Conclusion

If IDocs are created despite BD64 filters, it's due to this inherent SAP logic. To fully prevent unwanted IDoc generation, implement Badi IDOC_CREATION_CHECK as recommended in SAP Note 822929. This ensures only relevant IDocs are generated, optimizing data processing.

entry.title: SAP Community CWB no Business Round Event – 44ª edição
entry.content:
No último Business Round Event, a SAP Community Curitiba marcou presença com a participação dos nossos curadores, Adriano e Luiz.

 

 

O evento, reconhecido por conectar profissionais e fomentar discussões estratégicas sobre tecnologia e negócios, foi uma excelente oportunidade para troca de experiências e networking.

Durante a 44ª edição, temas relevantes de inovação foram abordados, promovendo insights valiosos para a comunidade. A presença da SAP Community CWB reforça o compromisso em fortalecer a colaboração e compartilhar conhecimento entre os profissionais da região.

Se você participou do evento, compartilhe nos comentários os seus aprendizados e percepções.

Queremos continuar essa conversa e levar ainda mais conteúdo relevante para a nossa comunidade!

📍 Fique de olho nos próximos eventos e continue conectado com a SAP Community Curitiba!

#SAPCommunity #SAPCommunityCWB #BusinessRoundEvent #Networking #Inovação

entry.title: SAP Badges: Reconhecendo sua Participação em Eventos da Comunidade
entry.content:
A SAP Community é um espaço vibrante onde profissionais compartilham conhecimento, trocam experiências e se conectam. Para reconhecer a participação ativa dos membros, a SAP introduziu os SAP Badges, que são selos digitais concedidos por diferentes contribuições, incluindo a participação em eventos.

O que são os SAP Badges para Eventos?

Os SAP Badges para eventos são distintivos concedidos a membros que participam de eventos oficiais da comunidade SAP. Eles aparecem no perfil do usuário e servem como uma prova digital de engajamento e envolvimento na comunidade.

Como obter um SAP Badge de Evento?

• Participação ativa – Compareça a eventos oficiais da SAP Community, como SAP TechEd, SAP Inside Track, e eventos locais.

• Interação – Algumas vezes, os badges são concedidos a quem interage nas discussões do evento, faz perguntas ou compartilha insights.

• Registro na comunidade – Certifique-se de que seu perfil na SAP Community esteja atualizado e vinculado ao evento em questão.

Por que os SAP Badges são importantes?

• Reconhecimento profissional – Os badges são uma forma de destacar seu envolvimento com a comunidade SAP, agregando valor ao seu perfil profissional.

• Networking – Mostram que você participa ativamente, facilitando conexões com outros profissionais e especialistas.

• Motivação – Servem como incentivo para continuar aprendendo e contribuindo para a comunidade.

Exemplo de Badge de Evento

Na imagem acima, vemos um badge específico concedido por participação em um evento da SAP Community no Q4 2024. Esse tipo de reconhecimento é uma maneira de valorizar aqueles que investem tempo no aprendizado e na troca de experiências.

Conclusão

Os SAP Badges são mais do que simples ícones no perfil – eles representam aprendizado, envolvimento e reconhecimento.

Se você ainda não tem um, participe de eventos da SAP Community e comece a construir sua coleção!

entry.title: 🚀 Enquanto ainda não temos eventos locais, seguimos conectados ao SAP Business Campus em SP!
entry.content:
Nossa comunidade foi criada para fomentar conhecimento, networking e trocas de experiências sobre SAP em nossa região. Ainda estamos planejando nossos primeiros eventos locais, mas, enquanto isso, seguimos acompanhando o SAP Business Campus, que acontece em São Paulo, trazendo conteúdos relevantes sobre inovação, tecnologia e tendências do ecossistema SAP.

Fique ligado e participe das discussões por aqui! Em breve, esperamos trazer encontros e iniciativas presenciais para fortalecer ainda mais nossa comunidade. 💡

🔗 https://events.sap.com/br/business-campus-2025/pt/home 

#SAPCommunity #Networking #EventosSAP #SAPBusinessCampus

entry.title: Blog it Forward - Raquel Cunha - version 2025
entry.content:
After a long time since my last blog here, I’m back to the Community thanks to Sandra Solis, who blogged it forward to me. Here you can check her blog.

My name is Raquel Pereira da Cunha, better known as Raquel Cunha, and also known as RaquelSolMan on Twitter, which I stopped using since it became X. I’m a Brazilian and live in São Paulo. I'm an ABAP Developer who decided to add some functional skills when an opportunity appeared, and started learning and working as SAP Solution Manager Functional Consultant, with a little bit of Basis (you can’t be a good ChaRM consultant if you have no idea about Change and Transport Management, the first book from SAP Press I bought). Nowadays I work with SolMan and SAP Cloud ALM, and still develop whenever my projects need. 

Clip of a video where I shared my experience at an SAP TechEd event.

A few years ago, a very interesting challenge was launched on SAP Community, and had a huge success and engagement. Oh…, checking the date better, in fact that was not really a few, but several years ago! More than a decade now! WOW, time flies…

So, in Aug 2012 Moshe Naveh launched the Blog it Forward series, and I was invited by my good friend and fellow SAP Mentor Tom Cenens to participate. At that time blogging was not something very usual to me, and it took me a considerable time to complete the challenge, considering that I had to put all my ideas in place, in English (my native language is Portuguese), trying my best to make it an enjoyable read. That was a great challenge, and I suppose only the readers can confirm if I succeed. 😉

An important point to be mentioned is that at that time there was no AI tool to help in writing. No ChatGPT, or Copilot, or Claude, or Gemini, or DeepSeek, or Manus, or whichever tool we have been using lately. Just as I’m doing now, sitting again on the same chair at the same desk, I did it by myself. I probably used a translator for one or another phrase, but the text came from my heart and my mind, just as I’m doing now. Maybe that is the main reason why it took me so long to write. Ideas and emotions come and go out of our control, and while you are writing about something, suddenly another thing comes to mind, maybe not related, maybe so, and you have to put in words before you lose it. By the way, the first part of this blog came to my mind while I was lying down with my son helping him to fall asleep this evening.  

Well, back to the point, thanks to Sandra, and to Stephanie and Katherine who loved the idea of having a new version of the challenge, I’m here again, writing my 63rd blog. It’s a special moment because I was recently invited to rejoin the SAP Mentors group, after a few years away. It gave me the chance to meet virtually Stephanie and Katherine, and share the idea and the feeling I had at that time when I wrote my 1st BIF.

How being an SAP Community active member changed your life?

To answer this question, I will write about how beneficial it can be, to your professional and personal life, to be part of a community and engage with people. I will also share some facts that have happened since I wrote my first BIF. So many things happened thanks to this fantastic Community! 

When I wrote that BIF blog I was just starting my participation in the SAP Mentors group. Many things were new for me, such as blogging, attending SAP international events and local events. My first SAP TechEd was in 2010, in Berlin (yes, Berlin!! I love it. I really hope I can attend this year!). 

Entrance SAP TechEd Berlin

Then, one year later, in Madrid 2011, I was there again, and this time I was not only an attendee, but also a panelist in an event called Embracing Inclusion with Design Thinking – Driving Innovation. In this panel I told my history about being a woman in IT working abroad, travelling to many countries, speaking foreign languages and how these experiences changed my life and how I see the world. To travel is to live.

Embracing Inclusion panel: Patti Fletcher, Raquel Cunha, Thorsten Franz, Claudia Brack, Kerstin Geiger. Picture by Roel van den Berge.

That was the first of many events I attended. In 2012 I was nominated SAP Mentor due specially to the knowledge I shared with SAP Community, answering questions, writing technical blogs and articles for SAP magazines, and helping to organize local events. After that I could present sessions in other TechEds, SITs and User Group events, was co-speaker in hands-on sessions with product owners, who I had the great pleasure to meet in person, and with whom I still can have great discussions. 

 Picture of a session I presented at Teched, and the last SITSP I helped organize

I hosted some interviews for SAP TechEd Live. That was tough, because I’m not used to making videos, never felt comfortable in front of a video camera, and the interviews were live streamed for the whole world. I remember one of them where I made a mistake in the very beginning, and said “Sorry, can I start again?”. I restarted but whoever was watching it live could see it. 😬

SAP Community Network homepage in 2012 showing the interview with me and other 3 members about the power of SCN. 

Two of the Teched Live interviews I hosted: with Marc Thier, and with David Birkenbach and Tobias Hauk.

I also had a chance to sing a Brazilian song with the Jam Band during SAP TechEd 2013 in Amsterdam!  Who remembers the #GeeksCanDance SAP Jam Band? TechEd is not Education only. It’s networking and fun.

Picture in the left taken from the TechEd Gallery and in the right by Frank Koehntopp.

In 2017 SAP Mentors program completed 10 years, and of course we celebrated! There was a party at TechEd Barcelona and we all had a lot of fun. In the picture below I’m with Lucía Subatin and Meredith Hassett, both former SAP Developers.

In this Teched we also had a panel called #sheinnovates presented by SAP Next-Gen and Woman in Technology at SAP, in which the idea was to connect, educate, empower and celebrate female technologists together with the SAP Mentors, SAP TechEd and SAP Community organizations.

Members collaborating with each other

Before the pandemic I had the chance to work with two very well known SAP Community members, Lluis Salvador and Tom Cenens. Lluis and I had never met before, but we followed each other for a long time via SAP Community, Twitter and LinkedIn, and used to chat. When he once needed someone with my expertise to help in a project, he contacted me and I had the immense pleasure of working with him and his team in beautiful Barcelona for some weeks. That was another dream come true, the first was Italy, but that history is from the old BIF. TechEd was in Barcelona that year and happened while I was working there, as well as SAP ALM Summit (that one in Germany) and I attended it too. Those were amazing weeks. 

And the fact that Lluis contacted me shows how strong a community can be. We developed a nice and trustable relationship without knowing each other in person. In Barcelona I met him and Mireia, his wife, and we had a great time together. They were both very careful and attentive with me, and at the same time very fun.


  Picture by Lluis Salvador

After that experience I worked for 3 years with Tom, 100% remote, but I had the chance to spend a week in Belgium and meet him and the customer when I started. We used to meet only in SAP events before. That was another amazing experience, in which we could know each other better and learn from each other. It was a fantastic collaboration and kept us close, even physically far away, which we still are.

Community awards

SAP Community knows how to show its appreciation. I was awarded 3 times, 3 years in a row. 

  • I first received the Developer Heroes award for helping to bring Developers and Experts together. 
  • Then in another year I received the SAP Community Appreciation Award, voted by Community members due to my contributions and commitment. 
  • And last but not least, I received the Community Heroes award. I’m so grateful for these recognitions.

Look at how many things I experienced being part of this Community!

What source do you use the most for learning SAP technologies?

I was a huge fan of openSAP. I truly loved it, and the team knows it. That was the most fantastic learning platform for SAP people (consultants, customers or just curious people) ever in my opinion. It was free, on-demand, the first SAP platform which offered MOOCs, and you could learn anywhere, anytime, on any device. That was the slogan. The only other platform of MOOCs that I used before that was openHPI. As openSAP was sadly discontinued, and moved to Learning, I moved too. And I am really enjoying it. I’ve done lots of Learning courses, always following the exercises they provide in Enable Now videos or hands-on using the trial systems when offered. I also use help.sap.com a lot, and of course SAP for Me.  

What need does the SAP solution you work with meet?

I decided for fun to ask ChatGPT this question, considering SAP Cloud ALM, and this is the answer I received: 

"SAP Cloud ALM is like a personal trainer for your SAP systems—it keeps your projects in shape, your operations running smoothly, and prevents your IT from having a meltdown. No more chaos, just efficiency and peace of mind!"  

Do you agree? 😀  No more chaos in projects… That sounds totally hallucinating 😆 But it can indeed provide essential guidance to customers, especially those who want to adopt a clean core. It's designed to fit the cloud-mindset, so if you still believe it's going to have everything provided by SolMan in the same way, forget it, because it's not going to happen. But it's made to be simpler and to focus on what’s essential for implementation and ongoing operations. 

Fun facts about me

  • I’m a super fan of Måneskin. 😎
  • I used to create bijoux with silver, beads and stones in my free time between my SAP contracts. I’m so electric, but when creating these pieces I could relax and stay sitting there for hours, placing the beads one by one very carefully.  Of course I had a pause because in my free time now I prefer to play with my son. We both love Lego and puzzles, so we spend a lot of time putting them together. 
  • A few years after my first BIF, I started working 100% remote from Brazil. Remote working was already a reality for me, since I spent some years working part-time onsite in Europe, and part-time from home in Brazil, after many years working abroad. So when the pandemic changed the way of working for almost everyone, I was already used to it. It was my way of working. But I was not used to being stuck at home, and with a baby.  When we were starting to go out with my son, taking him to a park, walking around, visiting family, then the pandemic started. 
  • When I was in University, my dream was to continue my studies in the University of Salamanca. I never accomplished that mission, but I finally visited the University in 2018, just when it completed 800 years. And it was amazing! I was so happy to be there, to walk inside and get into the rooms. 

  • I once visited Alan Turing’s office in Hut 8 at Bletchley Park. That was one of the highlights of all my trips. If you have a chance and interest (let’s check how geek you are), Bletchley Park is a short 45-minute train ride from London.

  • I was one of the supporters who helped the book Saving Bletchley Park, by Dr. Sue Black, to happen. It was funded by readers. I recommend this reading. It tells how social media saved Bletchley Park, the home of WWII codebreakers. I once met Dr. Black in a Women in IT event in São Paulo. She is amazing. She created BCSWomen, the UK’s first online network for Women in Tech, besides many other fantastic things she’s done for Women in IT. She received lots of awards, including the OBE -  Order of the British Empire - for services to technology. If you are a woman in IT, follow her! In the picture below I’m with Estela Dantas, a good friend and SAP professional too, Camila Achutti, CEO of Mastertech and a world reference for Woman in Tech, and Dr. Black.

  •  I enjoy silence and being alone. I think the remote work made me start enjoying my own company more than ever. Of course it’s not all the time, but I really need it from time to time. I like to walk or to spend some time in a coffee place reading, listening to podcasts or even doing nothing, only observing.
  • I'm a little bit geek, but I like Design Thinking, which helped me to see things in a different perspective, specially when creating solutions. And, after finishing a DT course, I had the chance to be coach at least in 2 opportunities, and one of them was in an SAP Innojam at TechEd 2013. As my love for travelling and my curiosity never end, always willing to learn more, I visited the famous HPI School of Design Thinking in Postdam, and had the pleasure of attending one class with a friend who was a student there. That was an amazing experience.
  • There is a song, among many, that I identify with very much when I think about my younger self. Here is a part of it:

I'll spread my wings and I'll learn how to fly
I'll do what it takes till I touch the sky
And I'll make a wish, take a chance, make a change
And breakaway

I wanted to share how life can be changed when you are part of a community, being honest and open to ideas and opportunities. Nowadays there is so much fakeness everywhere, people pretending to be something they are not, to be experts in areas they don’t really have a clue, using AI tools that hallucinate and they don’t even notice before posting. You can’t build strong professional and personal relationships without being true to yourself, at least not for a long time. And why am I saying these things? Because I lived enough to see it. 😉

Take a chance and make a change. 

Concluding my writing for today, I just want to say:

 “I’m still here”. 

“Cobra Kai never dies”. Well, I like the Karate Kid franchise too. 😄

I would like to Blog it forward to @Lluis Salvador, who has just became an SAP Champion after many years collaborating with the SAP Community. I’m sure he has many interesting things to share with us.

 SAP Cloud ALM SOLMAN Change Control Management SAP Solution Manager

entry.title: Community Member Spotlight: Manuel Robalinho - March 2025 Edition
entry.content:
You might have seen our first four iterations of recognizing active authors (one / two / three / four)  in SAP Community. I am thrilled to share our fifth interview with @MRobalinho (a SAP Champion) with you which I conducted recently. Read more about his background, his experience with organizing community events and what motivates him to contribute to SAP Community! 

 

Marco Santos (MS): Hello Manuel! Happy to have this interview with you. Let's start with our first question. How and when did you join the SAP Community?

Manuel Robalinho: My active participation in the SAP Community began in 2020 when I was invited by Marssel Vilaça to speak at SAP Inside Track Recife. Marssel has always been a major influence on me. He not only introduced me to how the SAP communities work but also provided full support in organizing our first event in Fortaleza.

Before that, I had already been working with SAP products for quite some time, but my connection with the community really started to strengthen during this period. While working on a project in Recife, I had direct contact with Marssel and saw how engaged he was. He has a unique ability to bring people together and create a welcoming environment within the community. He was the one who encouraged me to submit a presentation proposal for the event in Recife at the beginning of 2020. As soon as I sent it, he responded immediately and gave me all the support I needed to participate.

After that event, the pandemic hit, and everything changed. At first, we weren’t sure how to continue, but within our circle of friends and colleagues, we started discussing ideas. We had a small group that used to meet for coffee before work, and it was in this group that the idea of bringing the event to Fortaleza emerged. We even joked that we could do it better than Recife!

So, in October 2020, we managed to organize the first SAP Inside Track Fortaleza, taking advantage of a brief window when pandemic restrictions were loosened. We carefully followed all the safety guidelines, ensuring proper distancing between chairs and other necessary precautions. It was a huge challenge, but also a significant achievement.

Since then, we never stopped. We continued organizing events and strengthening the local community. Marssel has always been there, supporting all our events, traveling specifically to join us in Fortaleza, and sharing his knowledge about the community. It was thanks to that first step and my engagement with him that we were able to build something solid and keep our community active.

MS: What does the SAP Community mean to you?

Manuel Robalinho: For me, the SAP Community is all about sharing knowledge and building strong professional connections. I genuinely enjoy teaching and guiding newcomers, helping them understand how things work, rather than keeping knowledge to myself. Holding onto what I know has never really benefited me—true growth happens when we share.

It’s interesting because sometimes we think we know very little, but when we share our insights, others recognize the value in our experience. I believe that by passing on what I know, I contribute to the community while also learning from those around me. Of course, there will always be people who know more, but they also had their chance to step up and share their expertise.

Beyond knowledge-sharing, one of the things I love most about the SAP Community is reconnecting with former colleagues and friends from past projects. The atmosphere at community events is so much more welcoming and engaging compared to large conferences, where you simply visit booths without knowing anyone. SAP Community events create a warm, collaborative environment where you’re not just networking—you’re building real relationships that last beyond the event itself.

MS: What is your experience with SAP?

Manuel Robalinho: My main expertise and experience lie in the realm of SAP solutions, specifically within the Finance and Controlling modules. I am certified in Finances S/4HANA, a specialization that has greatly shaped my professional journey. Initially, when I was based in Europe, my focus was slightly more on FI (Financial Accounting), but upon moving to Brazil, my role evolved, and I became heavily involved with the CO (Controlling) module, partly due to the specific needs and materials available here.

Throughout my career, I've worked extensively as a consultant in CO, leading various projects and teams. Still, my background and keen interest in FI have remained integral to my work, especially as I collaborate on developing partnerships with SAP implementers, mainly within financial sectors. This involves pioneering connectivity solutions between SAP systems and financial institutions, dealing with bank layouts and other integration necessities.

Additionally, my curiosity and passion for technology drive me to explore areas such as AI, Machine Learning, and Python development. Also, in recent times, I've delved into SAP Analytics Cloud, particularly concerning budgeting processes, which have increasingly migrated to this platform. This has necessitated a deeper understanding of technological capabilities and limits, ensuring that our solutions are both practical and forward-thinking. Therefore, my expertise primarily revolves around FICO, focusing on budgeting and related financial solutions.

MS: Is there any highlight you would like to share?

Manuel Robalinho: I am part of the organization team for the SIT Porto event, and it's been an incredible experience to contribute my expertise in organizing SAP community events. Last year's event was notably a high point for us. Initially, we started with a venue that could accommodate around 100 people, but as the years progressed, the demand increased, and we had to adapt. The year prior, the high point was renting a space by the Iracema beach where we had anticipated 300 attendees. We were thrilled when tickets sold out, and even our contingency plan allowed for additional participants beyond expectations. However, hosting a coffee break for such a large crowd was a logistical challenge that taught us valuable lessons.

Our success brought a sense of joy seeing the enthusiastic participation but also highlighted the need for larger venues, as guests often exceed the expected numbers, including sponsors and volunteers. It's fulfilling to see many attending, but it was crucial to learn from these moments to improve future events. After every event, we conduct a ‘lessons learned’ meeting to identify what worked and what needs adjustment. Our aim for the 2025 event is to refine our approach, ensuring our SAP Porto community sets itself apart, adapting to both local contexts and technological needs.

MS: Do you have any plans for your future in the SAP Community?

Manuel Robalinho: From my perspective, the enthusiasm for these events is only bound to increase, both from the SAP Community and the consulting firms that sponsor them. These events offer excellent lectures and showcase impressive case studies. Almost all participants know each other, and they're organized with relatively low costs. This is precisely what we need to make these events appealing, both for sponsors and for the SAP consultants attending.

MS: On the other hand, what would you like to gain from the SAP Community or other community members?

Manuel Robalinho: Typically, I ask members to share their cases and knowledge by giving a presentation, whether onsite or online. I've successfully encouraged numerous individuals to deliver their first presentation. They enjoyed the experience and have continued doing it. It's these small achievements, the thrill of giving a first talk and developing an appetite for sharing, that I truly love to witness.

In my current role, I am required to engage with many people and clients, and this often involves a lot of meetings about partnerships at my new company. It's fascinating because, in many of these meetings, even if I don't lead them, I often hear, "I know you, I saw you at the event we attended or the one we planned together." These moments help break the ice in meetings because they recognize me as someone who is willing to share ideas and insights without any reservation.

Recently, I was called into a meeting where someone recognized me, which made it easier to connect. It became clear that the people I interact with understand that we're not just here for the money but to genuinely share what we're doing, offer tips, and show our project progress. This helps in forming more amicable and easygoing relationships. It's quite rewarding when others acknowledge that willingness to share knowledge openly, knowing that everyone is gaining from it

MS: Is there anything else you would like to share?

Manuel Robalinho: The support from the SAP team for community engagement has been outstanding and very welcomed. It's already bearing fruit, as evident by the number of such events taking place in Brazil in 2025. Your efforts and the 'reorganization' you implemented in terms of groups, influencers, and participation in events are excellent. I have honestly witnessed your work, and despite not knowing how things were coordinated in the past, it has been very beneficial. Congratulations to you and the team!

Your involvement and engagement with companies and community leaders have made a significant difference. It has allowed us to overcome barriers and given us the credibility to organize successful events. People now see the events marked on the SAP calendar as worthwhile, which gives reliability to the organizers.

Last year, I noted with great appreciation your participation in events, which was very beneficial as it provided immense credibility and helped engage many people. This, in turn, encourages more people to attend, participate, and contribute to events, whether through presentations or sharing ideas.

Additionally, actions like sharing small video clips or announcements about events, such as one promoting an event in Warsaw, have been effective in drawing attention and encouraging community engagement. This kind of partnership enhances the credibility of events and benefits everyone involved. SAP gains recognition for distributing product knowledge and brand awareness, while we benefit from increased participation and support in organizing our events, ultimately enriching the whole community. Furthermore, universities are now showing interest in engaging with SAP, which is very promising. Last year, they attended the event and were impressed, and now they're reaching out to me to explain more about SAP, showing how this engagement continues to expand positively.

MS: Thank you so much for having this interview with me and sharing your experiences!

entry.title: Bem-vindo ao Grupo Rio Grande do Sul - Bem vindos ao Grupo do Rio Grande do Sul!
entry.content:
You are in the right place if you want to collaborate with the other SAP enthusiasts in the state and Brazil. 

Here you will find all the information about SAP events in Rio Grande do Sul. We collaborate closely with SAP communities in Brazil, so we'll try to highlight all SAP Inside Tracks.

This place is also designed for keeping in touch with friends, sharing thoughts, and simply having fun.

Você está no lugar certo se quiser colaborar com outros entusiastas do SAP no estado e no Brasil.

Aqui, você encontrará todas as informações sobre eventos SAP no Rio Grande do Sul. Colaboramos de perto com as comunidades SAP no Brasil, então tentaremos destacar todos os SAP Inside Tracks.

Este lugar também foi criado para manter contato com amigos, compartilhar pensamentos e simplesmente se divertir.

 

Jarlei Nascimento

entry.title: SAP Inside Track RJ 2025 Summary – A Successful Event in Rio de Janeiro
entry.content:
SAP Inside Track RJ 2025 took place last Saturday at a hotel located in Botafogo with a stunning view of Guanabara Bay. The event had a packed amphitheater, highlighting the high level of engagement of the SAP community. The organization was impeccable, providing a pleasant environment with coffee breaks in the morning and afternoon, exclusive event t-shirts for all participants, and gifts offered by sponsors.

The event's great difference was the highly interactive lectures, which promoted enriching discussions among participants on relevant topics in the technical and functional areas. Among the topics that generated the most debate were Clean Core, SAP Grow, and the implementation of DRC. The most interesting thing was the diversity of perspectives, where each argument made sense within its context.

The program included high-level presentations, including:

  • Manuel Robalinho, presenting innovations on connectivity in the financial area and cash flow with AI;

  • Jonys Arcanjo, addressing news in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC);

  • Marssel Vilaça, speaking about SAP Community engagement;

  • Renan Correa, providing insights on DRC and its implementation;

  • Fabio Dezan, with an excellent talk on Brazil Localization;

  • Luciano Casado, explaining the integration of Apps with SAP Grow;

  • João Secco, delving deeper into the concept of Clean Core;

  • Ely Barbosa, with a practical and fun class on project management and common mistakes that should be avoided.

  • João Secco, delving deeper into the concept of Clean Core;

After a day of learning and sharing experiences, the participants ended the event at a nearby bar, where they continued the conversation in a relaxed atmosphere.

Congratulations to the organization for providing such an enriching event for the SAP community in Rio de Janeiro! 🚀👏

entry.title: O que o Linkedin mostrou sobre o #SITRIO2025
entry.content:
Este foi um evento para ficar na memória para sempre.

O Rio de Janeiro já havia sediado seu primeiro SAP Inside Track em 2011, tornando-se a segunda cidade do Brasil a receber o maior evento da comunidade SAP. Desde então, tivemos outras edições em diferentes formatos, como Meetups e novos SAP Inside Tracks. Mas a edição de 2025 foi diferente. Uma verdadeira reinvenção do SAP Inside Track.

 

Com uma equipe de organização super dedicada, esta edição foi totalmente remodelada e entregou o melhor evento SAP não-oficial já realizado na Cidade Maravilhosa. Parabéns demais a todos os envolvidos!

 

Agradecimento aos patrocinadores

Obrigado a todas as grandes marcas do mercado SAP que nos apoiaram. Nada disso seria possível sem essa grande ajuda na disseminação do conhecimento.

 

 

Conteúdo de Altíssimo Nível

As apresentações trouxeram os principais temas da atualidade no universo SAP, abordando:

Clean Core
SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Localization
Inteligência Artificial
Práticas fiscais e legais para o Brasil
Metodologias de projeto com dicas práticas

Os palestrantes deram um verdadeiro show à parte. Domínio técnico, carisma e conteúdo valioso marcaram cada fala. E a melhor parte? Você pode conferir a gravação completa do evento no YouTube. As apresentações estão disponíveis individualmente por meio dos links na descrição do vídeo. Imperdível!

Lista de Palestras:

00:00:00 Abertura do SAP Inside Track Rio de Janeiro 2025

00:08:52 ➤ Marssel Vilaça ‪@marssel700‬: Comunidade SAP

00:19:01 ➤ João Secco ‪@moovieducation‬: Clean Core para Funcionais - Como proceder?

01:50:18 ➤ Manuel Robalinho ‪@sitfor-sapinsidetrackforta6071‬ : Inovações nas interfaces bancarias

02:33:38 ➤ Jonys Arcanjo: SAP Analytics Cloud: Soluções Inteligentes para Negócios Orientados por Dados

03:11:01 Painel de Perguntas & Respostas: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud

03:34:10 ➤ Luciano Casado: Pagamento com cartões integrado ao SAP S/4HANA

04:17:23 ➤ Fabio Dezan ‪@FabioDezan‬: Mitos e Verdades sobre a Nova Localização Brasil e o Fim da TAXBRA no S/4HANA Public Edition

05:07:33 ➤ Lucas Ferreira: Como extrair o melhor do SAP com SAP Fiori e personalizações Clean Core.

06:25:48 ➤ Ely Barbosa: O que não fazer em projetos de implantação SAP

07:21:04 Sorteio SAP Press

07:24:20 ➤ Renan Correa ‪@s4cn‬: Onde o caldo entorna - Como superar os maiores desafios na implementação do DRC NFE

08:15:32 Sorteio Final de Brindes & SAP Press

08:19:55 Encerramento

 

A Repercussão nas Redes: O LinkedIn Foi à Loucura!

Antes, durante e depois do evento, o LinkedIn explodiu com postagens, fotos, vídeos e feedbacks. A empolgação era visível, as expectativas foram superadas e o alcance foi simplesmente fantástico.

Foi emocionante acompanhar tantos comentários carinhosos, elogios sinceros e depoimentos de profissionais de altíssimo nível. Gente que vive, respira e transforma o ecossistema SAP todos os dias. O sentimento de comunidade esteve presente em cada palavra.

A seguir, compartilho alguns dos melhores momentos extraídos diretamente do LinkedIn. Uma pequena amostra do impacto que o SAP Inside Track Rio de Janeiro 2025 deixou em todos nós.

 

Desafios e agradecimentos da Organização

Michelle Batista

Fofuras em excesso para relembrar o SIT Kids❤️

 

A voz do SIT RIO - O nosso Mestre de Cerimônia

Eduardo Palafoz

 

Hora do Almoço com vista para a Enseada de Botafogo! 

Rodrigo Silva

 

O que disseram os Palestrantes ?

Jonys Arcanjo

Luciano Casado

Ely Barbosa

João Secco

Lucas Freitas

Manuel Robalinho

Renan Correa

 

 

Colaboração fantástica dos Voluntários à Organização 

Milena Boniolo

 

Experiêcia adquirida pelos participantes

Ruy Castro

Marcelle Freitas

Rodolfo Doerl

Gabriel Galdino

Hellen Santana

Luciana Xavier

Adriana Brito

Thiago Neves

Gabriela Silva

Filipe Suarez

Oberdan Silva

Luiz Cesar Kuhnert

SAP Press Winners!

 

www.sitbrazil.com

entry.title: Call for Speakers: SAP Inside Track Hamburg on Sept 27, 2025
entry.content:
🎤We are calling on consultants, developers, innovators, and thought leaders to contribute to the lineup of speakers at the SAP Inside Track Hamburg 2025. Share your insights in front of a tech-savvy audience and make an impact in the SAP community.

Submit your session proposal until July 13 here: https://forms.gle/FxHz6hSHGK8oXGoq5 

Event Details:

Date: September 27, 2025
Where: SAP Office Hamburg, Tesdorpfstraße 8, 20148 Hamburg
Session Proposal Deadline: July 13, 2025

Agenda

Put yourself on the Agenda! This is an SAP community event by the SAP community for the SAP community, packed with sessions from the SAP community. We offer session slots of 30 min or 45 min length, but you can optionally ask for a different length and we will get back to you.

Why to speak at SAP Inside Track Hamburg 2025?

  • Showcase Your Expertise: Share your knowledge with a passionate and engaged audience of SAP professionals.
  • Network with Peers: Connect with fellow experts, partners, and SAP enthusiasts.
  • Gain Recognition: Enhance your professional profile and gain recognition within the SAP community

What is an SAP Inside Track?

An SAP Inside Track is a local grassroots community event where SAP Community members come together to share knowledge about SAP related topics. The event is driven/organized by the community, no cost/entry and very informal: everyone who is interested may take part. Everyone can profit or contribute: act as presenter, take part in discussions, exchange information, realize networking or enjoy the social get-togethers in the evening.

We are looking forward to receiving your session proposals!

Your SAP Inside Track Hamburg 2025 organizer team

@Mark_Teichmann @hgoerke @christoph_christophersen @peter_langner @JudithFriedrich

entry.title: Exporting SAP Table Data as CSV and ZIP on Application Server using ABAP - A Comprehensive Guide
entry.content:
​In many SAP projects, there's a frequent need to extract master data or transaction data from the system for purposes such as reporting, data migration, or integration with external systems. A common approach involves exporting this data into a CSV file, compressing it into a ZIP archive, and saving it on the application server for efficient storage and transfer.

This blog post presents an ABAP report that automates the extraction of data from the SFLIGHT table, generates a CSV file, compresses it into a ZIP file, and stores both files on the application server. This solution is particularly useful for scenarios requiring data extraction and compression for streamlined processing.

Business Scenario

Assume you have a requirement to export a list of flight bookings to share with an external partner or to store for auditing purposes. However, due to data sensitivity or storage limitations, the file needs to be compressed before sharing. This ABAP report handles:

  • Reading data from SFLIGHT table
  • Creating a well-formatted CSV file
  • Zipping the file using CL_ABAP_ZIP
  • Writing both CSV and ZIP to the application server

Process Flowchart

Here's an overview of the key steps involved:

  1. Start - Export Process

  2. User Input: File Path & Name

  3. Select Data from SFLIGHT Table

  4. Create CSV String

  5. Save CSV File to Application Server

  6. Convert to XSTRING

  7. Create ZIP using CL_ABAP_ZIP

  8. Save ZIP File to Application Server

  9. Show Success/Error Messages

  10. End - Export Process

 

ABAP Code Sample:  

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& Report YYCSVZIP
*& Author: Shravankumar Tiwari *
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
REPORT yycsvzip.

*** Data Declaratio **
DATA:
  gv_filename     TYPE string,
  gv_filename_zip TYPE string,
  gv_filepath     TYPE string,
  gv_csv_string   TYPE string,
  gv_zip_path     TYPE string,
  gv_xstring      TYPE xstring,
  gv_o_zip        TYPE REF TO cl_abap_zip. "Zip Utility

*** Constant declaration
CONSTANTS gc_zip TYPE char04 VALUE '.zip'.

* Selection screen
PARAMETERS:
  p_path  TYPE string OBLIGATORY DEFAULT '/tmp/' LOWER CASE,
  p_fname TYPE string OBLIGATORY DEFAULT 'A_sflight_export' LOWER CASE ##NO_TEXT.

* Exception handling
DATA:
  gs_x_bcs TYPE REF TO cx_bcs.

START-OF-SELECTION.
* Select data from SFLIGHT
  SELECT carrid,connid,fldate,price,currency
    FROM sflight
    INTO TABLE
    (gt_sflight) UP TO 50 ROWS
    ORDER BY PRIMARY KEY.                               "#EC CI_NOWHERE

* Prepare CSV header
  gv_csv_string = 'CARRID,CONNID,FLDATE,PRICE,CURRENCY'.

* Add data rows
  LOOP AT gt_sflight ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<gs_sflight>).
    gv_csv_string = gv_csv_string &&
      cl_abap_char_utilities=>newline &&
      |{ <gs_sflight>-carrid }| &&
      |,{ <gs_sflight>-connid }| &&
      |,{ <gs_sflight>-fldate }| &&
      |,{ <gs_sflight>-price }| &&
      |,{ <gs_sflight>-currency }|.
  ENDLOOP.

* Prepare filename and path
  gv_filename = p_fname && sy-datum && sbiw_c_underscore && rsbo_c_suffix-csv.
  gv_filepath = p_path  &&  gv_filename.

* Write CSV file
  OPEN DATASET gv_filepath FOR OUTPUT IN TEXT MODE ENCODING DEFAULT.
  IF sy-subrc = 0.
    TRANSFER gv_csv_string TO gv_filepath.
    CLOSE DATASET gv_filepath.
  ELSE.
    MESSAGE 'File could not be created' TYPE 'E' ##NO_TEXT.
  ENDIF.

* Create ZIP file
  CREATE OBJECT gv_o_zip.
  gv_filename_zip = p_fname && sy-datum && sbiw_c_underscore.
  gv_zip_path = p_path && gv_filename_zip && gc_zip.

  TRY.
* Convert string to xstring with explicit exception handling
      gv_xstring = cl_bcs_convert=>string_to_xstring(
        iv_string   = gv_csv_string
        iv_codepage = '4103'     " UTF-8 codepage
        iv_add_bom  = 'X'        " Add Byte Order Mark
      ).
*ADD Adds a File to a Zip Folder
      gv_o_zip->add( name    = gv_filename_zip
                     content = gv_xstring ).

*SAVE Creates a Zip File
      DATA(gv_zip_path_data) = gv_o_zip->save( ).

* Write ZIP file on apllication server
      OPEN DATASET gv_zip_path FOR OUTPUT IN BINARY MODE.
      IF sy-subrc = 0.
        TRANSFER gv_zip_path_data TO gv_zip_path .
        CLOSE DATASET gv_zip_path.
      ELSE.
        MESSAGE 'Zip File could not be created' TYPE 'E' ##NO_TEXT.
      ENDIF.

** Success messages Output
      DATA(gv_file_message) = | { gv_filepath } { cl_abap_char_utilities=>newline } { gv_zip_path }|.
      cl_demo_output=>display( gv_file_message ).


    CATCH cx_bcs INTO gs_x_bcs.
      " More detailed logging approach
      MESSAGE ID 'ZZZ' TYPE 'E' NUMBER 000
        WITH 'BCS Conversion Error' ##NO_TEXT
        RAISING error ##MG_MISSING.
  ENDTRY.

Example Output

Assuming default parameters and today’s date is 20250331, the output files would be:

  • CSV: /tmp/A_sflight_export20250331_.csv

  • ZIP: /tmp/A_sflight_export20250331_.zip

 

Conclusion

This ABAP code demonstrates how standard ABAP can be used efficiently for file handling, CSV generation, and ZIP compression — all without any third-party tools. It’s ideal for creating reports, system integrations, or any scenario requiring lightweight file exports.

entry.title: SAP TALK WOMEN CWB 2025: Conexões que Transformam
entry.content:
Na terça-feira, 18 de março, aconteceu a primeira edição do SAP Talk Women CWB 2025, um evento pensado para fortalecer a comunidade feminina no universo SAP. O encontro foi realizado no Eva Space Coworking, em Curitiba, e reuniu mulheres incríveis para uma noite de networking, aprendizado e inspiração.


Um início acolhedor
As portas se abriram às 18h30, e as participantes foram calorosamente recebidas pelos curadores da SAP Community Curitiba, @SimoneAzevedo  e @TiagoFelipe . Logo na chegada, cada uma recebeu um abraço e uma rosa azul, símbolo da SAP comunidade CWB e da feminilidade.

Para tornar o momento ainda mais especial, a recepção contou com uma mesa caprichada de frios, um welcome drink e uma trilha sonora envolvente, na voz da talentosa Marcela Juk. Durante a primeira hora do evento, as participantes puderam se conectar, trocar experiências e conhecer novas colegas da comunidade SAP.


Conteúdo Inspirador
Às 19h30, os curadores deram as boas-vindas oficiais, compartilhando a missão da SAP Community e destacando a importância de eventos como esse para o crescimento profissional das mulheres no setor. Logo após, foi a vez de Fernanda Chaves subir ao palco para conduzir a palestra "Empregabilidade Feminina no Mundo SAP: Conexões que Transformam Carreiras".
Com um discurso inspirador, Fernanda trouxe reflexões valiosas sobre as oportunidades e desafios que as mulheres enfrentam na área de tecnologia, ressaltando a importância do networking e do apoio mútuo para impulsionar carreiras.


Momentos de Relaxamento e Surpresas
Além do conteúdo enriquecedor, o evento também proporcionou um momento de autocuidado com quick massagem, garantindo que as participantes saíssem renovadas. E para fechar a noite com chave de ouro, cada participante recebeu uma linda taça personalizada, como lembrança especial desse encontro inspirador. Também tivemos um sorteio de brindes incríveis, gentilmente oferecidos pela patrocinadora do evento.

Até 2026!
O SAP Talk Women CWB 2025 foi um verdadeiro sucesso, reunindo mulheres inspiradoras e criando conexões valiosas. A energia do evento foi incrível, como você pode ver na foto abaixo!


Confira mais fotos do evento no link: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1f4vmhNNTgIHHwslfTandFKaq8qPmT6-9?usp=sharing

Agradecimento especial aos nosso patrocinadores, sem vocês nada disso seria possível. Nosso muito obrigada!!


E a melhor parte? Já estamos no calendário anual da SAP Community Curitiba! Se você perdeu essa edição, não se preocupe—nos vemos em 2026 para mais uma noite de trocas, aprendizado e empoderamento feminino!

#SAPTalkWomenCWB

entry.title: Migrating customers using the Fiori App: Migrate Your Data in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Migrating customers using the Fiori App: Migrate Your Data in SAP S/4HANA involves several steps to ensure a smooth and accurate data transfer. Below is a structured approach:

Steps to Migrate Customers Using Fiori App: Migrate Your Data

  1. Access the Fiori App
  • Log into the SAP Fiori Launchpad.
  • Search for and open the "Migrate Your Data" app.

   2. Create a New Migration Project

  • Click on "Create" to start a new migration project.
  • Choose the appropriate migration approach (File/Staging Table).

  • Enter a Project Name and select Local SAP S/4 HANA Database Schema and click on Step 2.

  • Select Development Package and click on Step 3.

  • Select the Customer Migration object.

  • Select the Customer Migration object “Customer” and click on Review button.

The selected objects will appear on the right side in the Selected Migration Object section. I just selected one object, but you can select as many objects as you need.

After completing the selection, go to Review

  • On Clicking on Review button, the below window popups, you can deselect the predecessor objects and click on Add.

  • On Create New Project click on Create Project.

  •  After creating the project, you will be directed to the main page of the Migrate Your Data app and you will be able to see the project you have created, in this case, the name I assigned is Sphoorthi BP Migration.

  • Click on the Project name created. Once you open the project, the object's info will be displayed. 

     3. Download the Migration Template

  • Click on Download Template to get an xml file with predefined fields.

  • Once you downloaded the XML file, open it with Ms Excel and fill all the fields with the data you want to migrate. Fill in the necessary customer details such as the below in the xml file and save.
    • Customer Number, Name, Address
    • Sales Area Data (Sales Org, Distribution Channel, Division)
    • Company Code Data (Reconciliation Account, Payment Terms)

     For the first test, it’s highly recommended that you fill just one line so that it will be easier to identify and      solve errors.

   4.Upload the Prepared Data File

  • Return to the migration project and upload the filled-out template.
  • Validate the data structure to ensure correctness.

Go back to Migration Project screen and click on Monitoring.

 

 

 

 

Select the Prepare button to start the process:

 

The status will be updated automatically, left-click on the option Mapping Tasks:

 

After clicking Prepare Staging tables, go back to Migration Project screen and click on Monitoring.

  1. Data Mapping and Validation
  • The system will automatically map most fields. If required, manually adjust mappings.

In this step, you will be required to confirm the values of the fields you inserted in the XML file.

  • You can select all the tasks on the left side and click on the Confirm to complete them at one time.

  • Another option is to click on each one and confirm the values.

 

 

  • Confirm All the values.

  • Resolve any errors before proceeding.

     6. Simulate the Migration

  • Run a test migration using Simulation Mode to identify potential issues.

  • The Simulation step will be ready, click to start:

    7. Execute the Migration

  • Once validation is successful, click Migrate Data to transfer customer records into SAP S/4HANA.
  • Monitor the migration status and logs for any errors or warnings.

  • Successfully Migrated.

    8. Verify Data in the System

  • Use transactions BP (Business Partner) or XD03/VD03 (Customer Display) to check migrated customers or tables BUT000, BUT100, BUT020 KNA1VV etc.
  • Ensure data consistency across Company Code, Sales Area, and General Data views.

    9.Final Confirmation

  • Once verified, mark the migration as Completed in the Fiori App.
  • Communicate successful migration to relevant stakeholders.

Conclusion

The Migrate Your Data Fiori app provides a user-friendly, guided approach to migrating customer master data efficiently. By following these steps, businesses can ensure data integrity and a smooth transition to SAP S/4HANA.

entry.title: SAP Inside Track Tokyo 2025 Event Report
entry.content:
SAP Inside Track Tokyo 2025 (sitTokyo2025)は、SAP技術の最新状況や自身の活動・経験を紹介するエキサイティングなイベントでした。3日間にわたって開催され、多様なSAP愛好者や専門家が集まりました。
SAP Inside Track Tokyo 2025 (sitTokyo2025) was an exciting event that showcased the latest advancements in SAP technology and provided a platform for participants to share their activities and experiences. The event was held over three days and attracted a diverse group of SAP enthusiasts and professionals.


イベント概要/Event Summary

このイベントは3日間に分けて行われ、それぞれ異なるSAPテーマとその応用に焦点を当てました。sitTokyo2025は、Connect Together, Better Together ～ ともに繋がり、ともに良くなる ～をコミュニティテーマに掲げ、今年も多くの有志によって開催をすることができました。イベント開催をサポートしてくれた運営メンバーの皆様、登壇してくれた皆様、本当にありがとうございました！ 今年のイベントでは、ユーザー企業からの登壇者が出てくれたこと、そしてJSUG (Japan SAP User Group)とのコラボ実現、新しいコミュニティメンバーの登壇と、これまでのコミュニティ活動の成果と今後の広がりを期待できる変化を感られました。これは大きな収穫でした。

The event was divided into three days, each focusing on different SAP themes and their applications.

sitTokyo2025 was held under the community theme "Connect Together, Better Together",  thanks to the efforts of many volunteers. We would like to extend our heartfelt gratitude to the organizing members who supported the event and the presenters who took the stage!  

This year's event featured speakers from user companies, collaboration with JSUG (Japan SAP User Group), and presentations from new community members, reflecting the achievements of past community activities and the promising expansion in the future.

３日間のイベントのスタッツは以下の通りです。
Here are the stats for the three-day event.

Attendee TTL: 307 / Session TTL: 16 / Event Satisfaction: 4.89 / 5.0pt

Date Track Topics
2/28 (Fri)Digital Transformation with Cloud ERP (DT) & Artificial Intelligence (AI)Attendees: 101
3/14 (Fri)Application Development and Automation (ADA) & Integration (IN)Attendees: 122
3/19 (Wed)Data and Analytics (DA)Attendees: 84

 

Sumikko さんが投稿したSAP Inside Track Tokyo 2025 Event Informationにも詳細が記載されていますので、より詳しい情報や動画をみたい方はそちらをご確認ください。

For detailed information about the event, please refer to the SAP Inside Track Tokyo 2025 Event Information posted by Sumikko. You can find information about the speakers and more there[^i^]. 

 

以下は各日の主なハイライトについてまとめておきます。
Below are the main highlights of each day.

Day 1

28th-Feb. Digital Transformation with Cloud ERP (DT)&Artificial Intelligence (AI)

セッションでは、SAUG-x / TechEd on tour Melbourneでの経験と学び、BTPデリバリーパートナー支援プログラム、RISE with SAPプロジェクトの悩み共有、JSUGテクニカル部会とASUG Tech Connectの紹介、Signavio Process Navigatorを使用したCloud ERPプロジェクトの推進法、ABAP開発における生成AI機能についての発表が行われました[^i^]。
The sessions included experiences and learnings from SAUG-x / TechEd on tour Melbourne, the BTP Delivery Partner Support Program, sharing challenges in RISE with SAP projects, an introduction to the JSUG Technical Committee and ASUG Tech Connect, promoting Cloud ERP projects using Signavio Process Navigator, and presentations on generative AI functionalities in ABAP development[^i^].

Day1 event youtube：http://youtu.be/1F2R8MJKv0Y

やはりAI関連のトピックスは関心が高くほぼ全てのセッションにAIに関するテーマがありましたね。内容はイベントに参加して得たSAPのAIに関する学びを帰ってから実際に試してみた話や、Jouleを日本のマーケットで利用できるようになるための情報収集から得た学びの共有、ABAP開発者の視点からのJouleへの期待、SAPのサポート部門でのAI活用事例など、多岐にわたる内容でした。それ以外にも若手の方がRISEプロジェクトで経験した学びについて共有いただいたり、Signavio Process NavigatorなどSAPが提供するプロジェクトツールの有効な活用方法などを共有いただきました。

Indeed, AI-related topics garnered significant interest, with almost all sessions featuring themes related to AI. The content ranged from sharing experiences of trying out SAP's AI learnings gained from the event, to discussing insights gathered for making Joule available in the Japanese market, expectations for Joule from the perspective of ABAP developers, and examples of AI utilization in SAP's support department. Additionally, young professionals shared their learnings from RISE projects, and effective utilization methods of project tools provided by SAP, such as Signavio Process Navigator[^i^].

登壇資料サマリー一例 /「Here are partial excerpts from session」

 


Day 2

14th-Mar.  Application Development and Automation (AD) & Integration (IN)

SAP Inside Track Tokyo 2025のDay2には、Clean CoreとBTPの両立実践、SAP Event Meshで始めるイベント・ドリブン・アーキテクチャ、S/4HANA・BTP開発技術者育成の取り組み、開発者拡張とIntegration Suiteを使ったインタフェース機能の開発、SAP BPAの最新機能の紹介、生成AI活用のためのPythonアプリ開発@Cloud Foundry runtimeなどのセッションが提供されました[^i^]。
On Day 2 of SAP Inside Track Tokyo 2025, sessions were provided on topics such as the practical implementation of Clean Core and BTP, starting event-driven architecture with SAP Event Mesh, initiatives for developing S/4HANA and BTP development engineers, developing interface functions using developer extensions and Integration Suite, introducing the latest features of SAP BPA, and developing Python applications utilizing generative AI on SAP BTP Cloud Foundry runtime[^i^].


Day2 Event youtube：https://youtu.be/ywP3LjdQ0FA

S/4HANA Cloudを導入し、Clean Coreを実践するために、どのような導入を意識するのか？システムの現状を可視化し最適な方法を選択するためのツールや手法を理解し、提案・デリバリできる人材を育成、BTPを利用した機能拡張・インテグレーションやビジネスAI活用のTipまで、統合的に理解できるものになっています。

To implement S/4HANA Cloud and practice Clean Core, it is essential to consider the approach to implementation. This involves visualizing the current state of the system, understanding tools and methods to select the optimal approach, and developing personnel who can propose and deliver solutions. The session covers tips for utilizing BTP for functional extensions, integrations, and business AI, providing a comprehensive understanding[^i^].

登壇資料サマリー一例 /「Here are partial excerpts from session」

 


Day 3

19th-Mar. Data and Analytics (DA)

SAP Inside Track Tokyo 2025のDay3では、AIを活用したSAP Analytics Cloudの「Just Ask」機能を使ったデータモデルの可視化、SAC/Datasphereの最新アップデート情報、SAP Signavio Process Insightsを使ったS/4HANAとERP 6.0の接続事例、そしてSAP TechEd 2024（メルボルン）でのData and Analyticsに関する学びが紹介されました。特に、実際のデモや具体的な手順を含むセッションが多く、参加者にとって非常に実践的で有益な内容となりました[^i^]。

On Day 3 of SAP Inside Track Tokyo 2025, sessions covered various topics including utilizing AI in SAP Analytics Cloud with the "Just Ask" feature for visualizing data models built on Datasphere, the latest updates on SAC/Datasphere focusing on planning functionalities, connecting SAP Signavio Process Insights with S/4HANA and ERP 6.0, and learnings from SAP TechEd 2024 in Melbourne centered around Data and Analytics. The sessions were highly practical and beneficial, featuring actual demos and detailed procedures[^i^].

Day3 Event youtube：https://youtu.be/lKFXUAeIGz4

コミュニティのベテランから若手、そして最も注目すべきはユーザー企業からも登壇者が出たことです。そしてその発表の内容も実際のプロジェクトに基づく素晴らしいものでとても価値の高いものでした。そしてここでもAIは重要なテーマとして取り扱われていました。

From seasoned community members to young professionals, and most notably, speakers from user companies also took the stage. Their presentations were based on actual projects and were highly valuable. AI was also treated as an important theme throughout[^i^].

登壇資料サマリー一例 /「Here are partial excerpts from session」

 

 

参加者のフィードバック / Feed Back from Audience

  • 全体的な評価 / Overall Evaluation:

    • 参加者はイベントを非常に楽しんだ様子で、「楽しかった」「最高でした」「勉強になりました」といったコメントが多く寄せられました。
    • 初めて参加した人々も「初めて参加しましたが、とても良かったです」「初参加でした、楽しかったです！」といったポジティブな感想をいただけました。
    • Participants seemed to thoroughly enjoy the event, with many comments such as "It was fun," "It was great," and "I learned a lot."
    • First-time attendees also shared positive feedback like "It was my first time attending, and it was very good," and "It was my first time, and I had fun!"

  • 学びと刺激 / Learning and Inspiration:

    • 多くの参加者がイベントを通じて新しい知識を得たことを強調しています。「勉強になりました」「新しい技術を扱えるプロジェクトに入りたい」「最新の技術キャッチアップできました」といったコメントが多く寄せられていました。
    • また、「良い刺激になりました」「技術トークはワクワクしますね！」といった感想もあり、イベントが参加者にとって刺激的であったことが伺えますね。
    • Many participants emphasized that they gained new knowledge through the event, with comments like "I learned a lot," "I want to join a project that handles new technology," and "I was able to catch up on the latest technology."
    • Additionally, there were remarks such as "It was a good stimulus," and "Tech talks are exciting!" indicating that the event was stimulating for the participants.

  • 運営と登壇者への感謝 /  Appreciation for Organizers and Speakers : 

    • イベントの運営に対する感謝の言葉も多く、また、登壇者に対しても「登壇者お疲れ様でした」「登壇緊張しましたが、楽しかったです！」といった感謝の言葉が多く寄せられています。運営として嬉しい限りです。
    • There were many words of gratitude towards the event organizers, as well as the speakers, with comments like "Thank you for the smooth operation," "Thank you to all the organizers," "Good job to the speakers," and "I was nervous about speaking, but it was fun!"

  • 次回への期待 / Expectations for the Next Event:

    • 多くの参加者が次回のイベントへの期待を示しており、「次は現地参加です！」「次は後輩連れて現地参加したいなぁ」といったコメントがありました、こんな人と人のつながりがコミュニティを強く大きくしていくんだなーと嬉しく思います。
    • また、「次の登壇ネタ作るぞ」「次は僕だ」といった意欲的なコメントもあり、参加者が次回のイベントに向けて積極的に準備を進めている様子が伺えますね。ぜひお待ちしてます！！
    • Many participants expressed their expectations for the next event, with comments like "Next time, I'll attend in person!" and "Next time, I want to bring my juniors and attend in person."
    • There were also enthusiastic comments like "I'll prepare the next presentation topic," and "Next time, it's me," showing that participants are actively preparing for the next event.

 

最後に / Wrap up

SAP Inside Track Tokyo 2025は、SAPの専門家や愛好者が知識を共有し、ネットワーキングし、SAP技術の未来を探求するための素晴らしいイベントでした。魅力的なセッションと洞察に満ちたプレゼンテーションは、SAPに興味のあるすべての人にとって必見のイベントでした。

SAP Inside Track Tokyo 2025 was a fantastic event where SAP experts and enthusiasts shared knowledge, networked, and explored the future of SAP technology. The engaging sessions and insightful presentations made it a must-attend event for anyone interested in SAP.

イベントの詳細な内容は、Sumikko さんが投稿したSAP Inside Track Tokyo 2025 Event Informationにもありますので、登壇者の情報などはそちらを参考にしてください。

For detailed information about the event, please refer to the SAP Inside Track Tokyo 2025 Event Information posted by Sumikko. You can find information about the speakers and more there.

このイベントのサマリーをお楽しみいただけたでしょうか。YouTubeリンクをチェックして、セッションをフルでご覧ください。次回のSAP Inside Trackイベントでお会いできることを楽しみにしています！

I hope you enjoyed this summary of the event. Check out the YouTube links to watch the sessions in full. We look forward to seeing you at the next SAP Inside Track event![^i^]

運営メンバーの皆さん、本当にありがとうございました。皆さんの一人一人の努力・支援に支えられ今年も素晴らしいイベントになったと思います。この場をお借りして改めて感謝したいと思います。

Thank you very much to all the members of the organizing team. I believe this year's event was wonderful, thanks to the efforts and support of each and every one of you. I would like to take this opportunity to express my gratitude once again.

entry.title: SAP Inside Track Campinas: knowledge, innovation, and community in action!
entry.content:
For those who don’t know yet, SAP Inside Track (SIT) events are independent community-driven gatherings, organized by SAP professionals to share practical knowledge, connect people, and promote innovation within the SAP ecosystem.

This year, the SAP Brazil community is on the move, with events confirmed in Rio de Janeiro, Campinas, Recife, Fortaleza, São Paulo, Salvador, and SIT Cerrado — a true knowledge tour across the country!

🌍And it doesn't stop there: in Portugal, the SAP Inside Track will take place on the last Saturday of September at The Lodge Hotel in Porto. A must-attend for anyone in the European SAP scene!

🔍What happened at SIT Campinas?

The event was packed with high-level content, with a strong focus on current and relevant topics:

🧠 Generative AI and AI Agents: Sessions explored how these technologies are being applied in the SAP landscape.
🤖Joule, SAP’s AI assistant: The intelligent assistant embedded into SAP Business Technology Platform was a hot topic throughout the day.
📊Tax Reform in Brazil: A pressing matter — we have until the end of 2025 to prepare SAP systems for the new tax model that takes effect on January 1st, 2026. A massive challenge for both tax and ERP teams.

🚀During my presentation, I shared how bank connectivity in SAP can be implemented using banking APIs, improving automation and security in financial processes.

And of course, keeping in line with the theme of the moment, I also introduced how Makevalue’s AI can be applied to Cash Flow Planning, showing how artificial intelligence can transform financial management.

👏Congratulations to all the amazing speakers — João Secco, Fabio Dezan, Thamires Kuwahara, Arthur Placido, Rodrigo Ramos Cordeiro, Luciano Casado da Silva Filho — and a big thank you to the organizing team and volunteers, especially Roberta Fernandes , who made this event possible.

 

🌟See you all soon at SAP Inside Track Salvador!
